etymology.description: Ultimately became ็นผ(็นผ) (jรฌ, "continue") with ็ณธ(็ณธ) (mรฌ, "silk; thread") as the semantic element
etymology.interpretation: The character ใกญ is an ancient form related to ็นผ(็นผ) (jรฌ, "continue"), serving as a phonetic component in the phono-semantic compound ็นผ, which involves ็ณธ(็ณธ) (mรฌ, "silk; thread") to suggest the idea of continuation or succession, as silk threads imply connection.
